cards.front: What does the type command do in Unix?
cards.back: The type command is used to determine the type of a command and locate it in the system.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the -a option with the type command?
cards.back: The -a option with the type command is used to locate the command in the /usr/bin directory.

cards.front: What does the -p option with the type command do?
cards.back: The -p option with the type command is used to print the path of the command.

cards.front: What does the type command return when a command has multiple types?
cards.back: The type command returns a message indicating that the command has multiple types and multiple locations.

cards.front: What is the default action of the type command if no option is provided?
cards.back: The default action of the type command is to locate the command in the system and return its type.
